text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members merge their code changes into a shared repository frequently. Each merge triggers an automated build, which compiles the code and runs a suite of tests. This process happens in what is known as a CI pipeline. The main goal is to catch issues early, before they grow into larger problems. By running tests automatically after every change, developers receive immediate feedback on whether their code works correctly with the rest of the project. This helps teams maintain high-quality software and reduce the time spent fixing bugs later.
